findings.title: Latent restoration network Rθ (Eq. 7/9) is absent; Stage-2 restores via VAE only
findings.claim: Stage-2 training forwards the degraded input self.lq only through net_g (a RAVAEHFLora, i.e. the LoRA-wrapped VAE encode→decode); no latent restoration network Rθ is instantiated or invoked anywhere in the repo.
findings.concern: The paper's restoration result depends on Rθ predicting a restored latent zres=Rθ(zdeg) (Eq. 7, and Eq. 9 runs HF(Dψ(Rθ(Eϕ(Ideg))))); with Rθ missing, the code merely reconstructs the degraded image with HF-LoRA and cannot produce the headline restoration numbers in Tables 1–5.
findings.resolution: Authors: please add the latent restoration network (paper says SFHformer; Table 5c also lists Restormer/NAFNet) and the LRes pre-training stage (Eq. 7), and show where zres = Rθ(zdeg) enters the Stage-2 pipeline.
findings.reason: Quote matches exactly at lines 132–139; _forward_g routes only through net_g (RAVAEHFLora) and no Rθ restoration network exists anywhere in the repo.

findings.title: Stage-2 LRes pre-training of Rθ with frozen VAE (Eq. 7) is not implemented
findings.quote:
def optimize_parameters(self, current_iter):
    loss_dict = OrderedDict()
    hf_gt = self._hf(self.gt)

    if self._is_fhf_step(current_iter):
        # FHF-LoRA: update encoder LoRA only.
        self._set_training_stage(train_enc=True, train_dec=False, train_disc=False)
        self.optimizer_enc.zero_grad()

        recon, posterior, z = self._forward_g()
        hf_pred = self._hf(recon)
        l_hf_fid = self.cri_pix(hf_pred, hf_gt) * self.lambda_hf_fid
        l_hf_fid.backward()
        self.optimizer_enc.step()

        self.output = recon
        self.posterior = posterior
        self.latent = z
        loss_dict['l_hf_fid'] = l_hf_fid
        loss_dict['stage'] = recon.new_tensor(0.0)
findings.claim: The VAEadapter trainer only alternates FHF-LoRA (encoder) and PHF-LoRA (decoder) HF-alignment steps; there is no phase that trains a restoration network with the standard restoration loss LRes = ||Dψ*(zres) - Iclean||1 (Eq. 7) before LoRA fine-tuning.
findings.concern: Eq. 7 (training Rθ with frozen VAE) is described as the first sub-step of Stage 2 and is the source of the restored latent; its absence means the described training protocol is not reproducible from this repo.
findings.resolution: Authors: provide the LRes pre-training script/config (which model, which loss, frozen-VAE setting) and how its checkpoint feeds the LoRA stage.
findings.reason: Quote matches exactly at lines 148–167; optimize_parameters contains only FHF-LoRA and PHF-LoRA branches with no LRes restoration pre-training phase.

findings.title: No evaluation/inference entrypoint and no metric computation for Tables 1–5
findings.quote:
from copy import deepcopy

from basicsr.utils.registry import METRIC_REGISTRY
from .niqe import calculate_niqe
from .psnr_ssim import calculate_psnr, calculate_ssim

__all__ = ['calculate_psnr', 'calculate_ssim', 'calculate_niqe']
findings.claim: The repo ships only basicsr/train.py; there is no test.py / inference / benchmark script, the two provided configs set `metrics: ~` (no metrics), no config evaluates a trained model on UHD-LL/UHD-blur/etc., and no FID metric exists at all (paper Table 3 reports FID).
findings.concern: Every PSNR/SSIM/LPIPS/FID/NIQE/user-study/FLOPs/runtime number in Tables 1–5 and Figure 2 is therefore untraceable to code that computes it, so none of the reported results can be reproduced or verified.
findings.resolution: Authors: add the evaluation entrypoint(s) and metric configuration used to produce each table (including FID), with the exact commands and the trained checkpoints.
findings.reason: Quote matches at lines 1–7; no test.py, inference script, or FID metric found anywhere in the repo; only train.py is present as an entrypoint.

findings.title: No dependency spec, no dataset/weights download, empty README, missing checkpoints
findings.claim: README.md is a single header line; there is no requirements.txt/setup.py/environment.yml, no dataset-prep or download script (configs hardcode ./datasets/train/{gt,lq}), and the referenced ./weights/dinov2_vits14.pth and ./weights/stage1_eqvae.pth are absent with no fetch instructions.
findings.concern: The environment cannot be rebuilt, the UHD benchmark data is not obtainable from the repo, the DINOv2 backbone and the Stage-1 VAE checkpoint are not provided, and there are no commands/results table — so the submission is not self-contained or runnable end-to-end.
findings.resolution: Authors: add a pinned dependency file, dataset acquisition instructions/scripts, the DINOv2 and Stage-1 checkpoints (or download links), and a README with the exact reproduce commands and a results table.
findings.reason: README.md contains only '# Latent-Harmony'; no requirements.txt, setup.py, or environment.yml found in the repo; absence confirmed by filesystem search.

findings.title: Stage-2 config requests vae_config.type=RAVAE, which is not a registered arch
findings.claim: The Stage-2 config sets the wrapped VAE type to 'RAVAE', but the only VAE class registered in ARCH_REGISTRY is 'RAVAE_EQ' (the Stage-1 config correctly uses RAVAE_EQ). RAVAEHFLora.__init__ calls ARCH_REGISTRY.get(arch_type), and Registry.get raises KeyError when the name is absent.
findings.concern: Stage-2 training crashes at network construction (KeyError: No object named 'RAVAE' found in 'arch' registry), so the provided Stage-2 config cannot run as shipped.
findings.resolution: Authors: change the type to RAVAE_EQ (or register a RAVAE class) and confirm the Stage-2 config runs.
findings.reason: Quote matches at lines 41–42; only RAVAE_EQ is decorated with @ARCH_REGISTRY.register() in LHVAE_arch.py, so ARCH_REGISTRY.get('RAVAE') will raise KeyError at runtime.

findings.title: Hardcoded author-machine absolute paths injected into sys.path and __main__
findings.claim: train.py (and LHVAE_arch.py line 7) append the author-only absolute path /fs-computility/ai4sData/liuyidi/code/LatentGen to sys.path; LHVAE_arch.py's __main__ further opens /fs-computility/.../options/debug.yml.
findings.concern: These author-specific paths do not exist on any other machine; while sys.path.append of a missing dir is silently ignored, it signals the code was not cleaned for release and the __main__ profiling path is dead, undermining the claimed FLOPs/Params profiling route.
findings.resolution: Authors: remove hardcoded absolute paths and make the profiling/debug entrypoints use repo-relative paths.
findings.reason: Quote matches at lines 7–8 of train.py; LHVAE_arch.py line 7 also appends the same path and line 467 opens the author-specific debug.yml in __main__.

findings.title: LEqv downsamples the perturbed latent, not zclean=Eϕ(Iclean) as in Eq. 5
findings.claim: The equivariance loss downsamples z, where z is the latent of the PDPS-perturbed input (optimize_parameters forwards self.pdps_input and reuses that z), whereas paper Eq. 5 defines zdown=Downs(zclean) with zclean=Eϕ(Iclean) from the clean image.
findings.concern: Using the perturbed latent instead of the clean latent is a valid-but-different equivariance constraint; it mildly couples the equivariance objective with the degradation perturbation rather than enforcing clean-image scale equivariance as written.
findings.resolution: Authors: clarify whether LEqv is intended on the clean latent (Eq. 5) or the perturbed latent; align code and paper.
findings.reason: Quote matches at lines 283–293; optimize_parameters passes z derived from self.pdps_input (perturbed) to _compute_eqv_loss, confirmed at line 330, not from clean image encoding.

findings.title: Shipped configs are *_example with toy batch sizes and no paper hyperparameters
findings.quote:
name: stage1_eqvae_example
model_type: EQVAEModel
num_gpu: 1
manual_seed: 123
findings.claim: Both configs are named *_example with batch_size_per_gpu: 2 and num_gpu: 1, and the paper's appendix is said to hold the actual training/eval setup (data splits, hyperparameters, compute) that is not present in the repo.
findings.concern: Without the real training configs (LoRA rank, loss weights, schedules, batch/GPU counts, dataset roots) the reported numbers cannot be reproduced; the example configs do not document the settings behind Tables 1–5.
findings.resolution: Authors: ship the exact configs used for each reported experiment, or point to where they live.
findings.reason: Quote matches at lines 1–4 of stage1_eqvae.yml; config is named *_example with num_gpu: 1, consistent with the finding that only toy example configs are shipped.
